SEDG.headline: Nasdaq 100 Drops, Intel Crashes 7% On Rate-Hike Panic: Stock Market Today
SEDG.description: U.S. equities declined Friday as rising oil prices and bond-market losses triggered rate-hike concerns, sending Treasury yields sharply higher. The Nasdaq 100 fell 1.6%, Russell 2000 dropped 2.4%, and S&P 500 declined 1.1%. Energy stocks rallied on crude surge, while tech and AI stocks suffered heavy losses. Intel crashed 7%, Tesla fell 4.3%, and NVIDIA dropped 3.5%. Precious metals collapsed amid dollar strength and rising real yields.

RDW.headline: What's Going On With Redwire Stock On Thursday?
RDW.description: Redwire shares surged 24.69% on Thursday following strong Q1 results (57.9% YoY revenue growth, $498.1M backlog) and news of a potential SpaceX-Google rocket-launch agreement for orbital data centers. Redwire continues work on NASA's Artemis program, providing camera systems and sun sensors for the Orion spacecraft.

NBIS.headline: Why Are Nebius Shares Surging On Thursday?
NBIS.description: Nebius Group (NBIS) shares surged 11.76% on Thursday following strong Q1 2026 earnings results. The company reported $399M in revenue (up 684% YoY) with AI cloud business revenue of $389.7M (up 841% YoY). A new $27B long-term agreement with Meta and analyst price target increases supported the rally. The stock is trading in overbought territory with RSI at 70.08, though the broader tech sector rally also provided tailwinds.

NBIS.headline: Better Datacenter Stock: CoreWeave (CRWV) or Nebius (NBIS)?
NBIS.description: CoreWeave and Nebius are both AI infrastructure companies experiencing rapid growth, but the article recommends Nebius as the better buy. CoreWeave has impressive revenue growth but faces widening losses and a high debt-to-equity ratio of 10.7. Nebius shows stronger growth prospects, lower debt (2.1 ratio), and is expected to achieve profitability by 2028, despite trading at a higher valuation multiple.

NBIS.headline: Nebius Upside Expands as AI Feedback Loop Intensifies
NBIS.description: Nebius Group benefits from an AI-driven feedback loop where infrastructure enables model training and inference, creating increasing demand. The company reported exceptional Q1 2026 earnings with nearly 700% revenue surge and significant margin expansion. While debt concerns exist, they are offset by strong cash position ($9B+), low leverage (<1X), and a robust pipeline with 4GW of contracted capacity. Analysts are catching up with bullish sentiment, though stock price is outpacing consensus est

NBIS.headline: Why Nebius Stock Jumped Today
NBIS.description: Nebius Group stock surged 15.88% after the AI cloud infrastructure provider raised its growth forecast, citing unprecedented demand for high-performance computing. The company is expanding into inference and agentic AI workloads through acquisitions, improving profitability with EBITDA reaching $129.5 million, and targeting over 4 gigawatts of power capacity by end of 2026 with plans for $7-9 billion in annual recurring revenue.

FLNC.headline: The AI Power Infrastructure Trade Has Never Been Stronger, But One Space Race Could Change That
FLNC.description: Hut 8 and Fluence Energy have secured major contracts positioning them as critical suppliers for AI infrastructure. Hut 8 signed a $9.8 billion 15-year lease at its Beacon Point campus, while Fluence qualified as a pre-qualified supplier to major hyperscalers for energy storage. However, emerging orbital data center projects from Google and SpaceX pose a long-term structural risk to ground-based power infrastructure investments, though economics remain unfavorable before 2030.

TSEM.headline: Why Tower Semiconductor Stock Skyrocketed This Week
TSEM.description: Tower Semiconductor surged nearly 30% this week following strong Q1 earnings that beat analyst expectations on both revenue ($413.6M, +15% YoY) and net income. The company also provided bullish Q2 guidance of $455M and announced $1.3B in silicon photonics contracts with major clients. Two analysts significantly raised their price targets, citing the company's strong growth potential in AI-related photonics technology.

TSEM.headline: Aeluma’s Post-Earnings Dip Creates a Buying Opportunity
TSEM.description: Aeluma (ALMU) experienced a ~35% stock price correction following Q3 earnings miss due to delayed government contracts, not fundamental issues. The company continues advancing its commercialization strategy with new partnerships for quantum dot laser manufacturing. Despite dilution concerns, analysts and institutions view the dip as a buying opportunity, with support levels near $23.50 and price targets around $25. Key catalysts include improving customer demand driven by AI and growing patent p

ONDS.headline: Why Ondas Stock Surged Today
ONDS.description: Ondas Holdings stock surged 26.52% after reporting exceptional Q1 2026 results with revenue jumping over 1,000% year-over-year to $50.1 million, driven by strong counter-drone system sales. The defense technology company has acquired Mistral and World View Enterprises, partnered with Palantir Technologies on an AI-powered intelligence platform, and projects full-year revenue growth of 670% to $390 million. However, the company remains unprofitable with widening EBITDA losses, though management e

RKLB.headline: 3 Defense Stocks to Buy in May
RKLB.description: With U.S. defense spending projected to exceed $1.5 trillion next year, three defense contractors are positioned for growth: RTX benefits from missile defense and aerospace demand; Honeywell is spinning off its aerospace segment focused on defense technologies; and Rocket Lab has secured major defense contracts for missile-defense satellites and hypersonic testing.

RKLB.headline: Karman: Defense Darling’s Outlook Strengthens After 40% Drop
RKLB.description: Karman, a defense company that went public in February 2025 at $22/share and peaked at $115 in January 2026, has dropped 40% but shows strengthening fundamentals. Q1 2026 results exceeded expectations with 51% YOY revenue growth to $151.2M and doubled adjusted EPS. The company raised full-year guidance, boasts a $1B backlog (61% YOY growth), and has 90% visibility into 2026 revenue. With new CEO Jon Rambeau's 30 years of defense industry experience and analyst price targets implying 65% upside,

RKLB.headline: Why Rocket Lab Stock Is Rocketing Higher This Week
RKLB.description: Rocket Lab stock surged to an all-time high of $133.18, rising 25.7% in early May following strong Q1 2026 results that beat analyst expectations with $200.4 million in revenue. Deutsche Bank raised its price target 64% to $120, while Clear Street initiated coverage with a $150 target. However, the stock trades at a steep 108x sales multiple, significantly above its historical 14.6x average.

RKLB.headline: Rocket Lab Targets Missile Defense and Golden Dome as Its Next Growth Market
RKLB.description: Rocket Lab announced a partnership with RTX Corporation to develop space-based interceptor missiles for the Golden Dome missile defense program, a $151 billion satellite-based missile shield initiative. The partnership positions Rocket Lab as a subcontractor on a $3.2 billion umbrella contract, leveraging its existing PWSA prime contractor status and its Electron and upcoming Neutron rockets for satellite launches. This partnership gives RTX a competitive advantage over Lockheed Martin and North

RKLB.headline: Rocket Lab Buys a Rival for $40 Million Cash
RKLB.description: Rocket Lab announced the acquisition of Motiv Space Systems for up to $60 million ($40 million cash plus $20 million in stock) to fill gaps in its vertical integration strategy. Motiv specializes in space robotics and precision mechanisms used on the Mars Perseverance rover. The analyst suggests Rocket Lab obtained a bargain, with the acquisition valued between 1.5 to 18.8 times sales depending on Motiv's actual revenue, significantly cheaper than Rocket Lab's own 86 times sales valuation.

LUNR.headline: Deal Dispatch: GoPro Retains Houlihan Lokey In Strategic Review, Brookfield Buys World Freight Company, YesCare Corp. Bankruptcy
LUNR.description: GoPro has retained financial advisers to explore a potential sale following unsolicited inquiries. Brookfield Asset Management acquired World Freight Company for $1.2 billion. Multiple companies announced strategic reviews including Neuphoria Therapeutics and XBP Global Holdings. YesCare Corp. and Spanish Broadcasting System filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy. Other notable deals include Allegiant's $1.5 billion acquisition of Sun Country Airlines, LVMH's sale of Marc Jacobs for $850 million, and C

LUNR.headline: Better Space Stock: Firefly Aerospace vs. Intuitive Machines
LUNR.description: Following SpaceX's announced IPO in July 2026, space stocks have rallied. The article compares two space companies: Firefly Aerospace, which builds rockets and lunar landers but faces direct competition with SpaceX's superior track record, and Intuitive Machines, which focuses on space infrastructure and satellites. Intuitive Machines is recommended as the stronger investment choice due to its NASA contracts, more experienced spacecraft operations, and closer path to profitability despite higher

KLAR.headline: Will S&P 500 Open Up Or Down On May 14?
KLAR.description: The S&P 500 climbed to a record high on Wednesday, rising 0.58% to 7,444.25, driven by semiconductor and AI-linked stocks despite hotter-than-expected inflation data. The May 14 Polymarket contract implies a 77% probability of the index opening higher on Thursday. Tech stocks, particularly semiconductors, continue to outperform amid AI demand optimism, though the rally remains narrow with two-thirds of S&P 500 stocks finishing lower.

POET.headline: Why Poet Technologies Stock Is Plummeting Today
POET.description: Poet Technologies stock fell 9.4% after reporting Q1 results that beat sales expectations but posted a larger-than-expected loss of $0.08 per share. The company announced a $400 million capital raise through the sale of 19.05 million shares and warrants at $21 per share, which will dilute existing shareholders' positions.

POET.headline: Why Is POET Technologies Stock Trending Overnight?
POET.description: POET Technologies (NASDAQ:POET) surged 11.28% in after-hours trading after reporting improved Q1 results with a net loss of $0.08 per share versus $0.32 in Q4, and triple the revenue year-over-year. The rally was fueled by a $50 million initial purchase order from Lumilens with potential to scale to $500 million over five years, plus new partnerships with Lessengers and LITEON Technology. The stock has gained 358% over 12 months and is trading near its 52-week high.

POET.headline: POET Technologies Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
POET.description: POET Technologies reported Q1 2026 revenue of $503,389 and a net loss of $12.3 million per share. The company announced major strategic partnerships with LITEON, Lessengers, and Lumilens to develop next-generation optical modules for AI data centers. Notably, Lumilens placed a $50 million initial purchase order with potential for $500+ million over five years. POET also announced plans to relocate its headquarters to the U.S.

POET.headline: POET CLASS ACTION ALERT: Johnson Fistel Encourages POET Technologies Inc. Shareholders to Submit Their Information Before June 29, 2026 Lead Plaintiff Deadline
POET.description: A class action lawsuit has been filed against POET Technologies Inc. on behalf of shareholders who purchased securities between April 1-27, 2026. The lawsuit alleges the company made false statements regarding its tax status as a passive foreign investment company, misrepresented its investment attractiveness, and that an executive violated a non-disclosure agreement by discussing business agreements publicly. Shareholders have until June 29, 2026 to become lead plaintiffs.

POET.headline: Why Poet Technologies Stock Is Skyrocketing Today
POET.description: Poet Technologies stock surged 33.96% after announcing a major contract with Lumilens worth potentially over $500 million over five years, with an initial $50 million hardware purchase order. This marks Poet's largest-ever contract win and boosts investor confidence following the dissolution of its Marvell Celestial AI contract. However, uncertainty remains about the final deal size and Lumilens' actual purchasing commitments.

POET.headline: Is This $14 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Meme Stock About to Go Parabolic?
POET.description: Poet Technologies shares have surged over 100% in one month amid speculation about its role in AI infrastructure through photonic interconnects. However, the company generated only $1.1 million in revenue in 2025 with $30 million in operating losses, resulting in an extreme P/S ratio of 949. While the optical interconnect market presents significant long-term potential, Poet's lofty valuation leaves little margin for error, making it a high-risk speculative play unsuitable for most investors.

POET.headline: POET CLASS ACTION ALERT: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Announces that a Class Action Lawsuit Has Been Filed Against POET Technologies, Inc. and Encourages Investors to Contact the Firm
POET.description: A class action lawsuit has been filed against POET Technologies, Inc. for allegedly making false and misleading statements regarding its tax status as a passive foreign investment company (PFIC), which could negatively impact U.S. stockholders. The complaint also alleges that an executive violated a non-disclosure agreement by discussing business agreements publicly. Investors who purchased POET securities between April 1-27, 2026 can join the lawsuit by June 29, 2026.
